light_analysis.conclusion: 本文的主要贡献是明确指出了开放结束AI系统特有的安全挑战，并论证了这些挑战须预防性解决，而非事后应对。研究具有重要的学术和实际价值，为AI安全领域开辟了新方向。未来工作可能包括设计新的安全框架和治理策略，以支持开放结束AI的负责任部署。

title: Understanding and Mitigating Bias Inheritance in LLM-based Data Augmentation on Downstream Tasks
abstract: Generating synthetic datasets via large language models (LLMs) has emerged as a promising approach to improve LLM performance. However, LLMs inherently reflect biases in their training data, leading to a critical challenge: when models are trained on synthetic data, they may propagate and amplify the inherent biases that can significantly impact fairness and robustness on downstream tasks-a phenomenon we term bias inheritance. This work presents the first systematic investigation in understanding, analyzing, and mitigating bias inheritance. We fine-tune LLMs with a combined dataset of real and LLM-augmented data with varied bias ratio as the proportion of augmented data. Through systematic experiments across 10 classification and generation tasks, we analyze how 6 different types of biases manifest. Our results indicate that bias inheritance harms downstream task performance in bias directly-related classification and generation tasks. Then, our analysis identifies three key misalignment factors: misalignment of values, group data, and data distributions. Based on these insights, we propose three mitigation strategies: token-based, mask-based, and loss-based approaches, which can work differently on various tasks and bias, indicating the substantial challenges to mitigate bias inheritance. We hope this work can provide insights to the research of LLM data augmentation.
comment: ACL 2026 Main Conference; code available at https://github.com/MiaomiaoLi2/bias-inheritance

light_analysis.overview: 首次系统研究LLM数据增强中的偏差继承现象，提出三种缓解策略。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM生成合成数据时，会继承训练数据中的偏差，导致下游任务公平性和鲁棒性受损，即偏差继承问题。现有研究缺乏对此现象的系统理解、分析和缓解方法，因此亟需探索偏差继承的机制及应对策略，以提升LLM数据增强的可靠性。
light_analysis.method: 通过混合真实数据和LLM增强数据（改变增强数据比例），在10个分类和生成任务上微调LLM，分析6种偏差类型的表现。识别出三个关键错位因素：价值观错位、群体数据错位和数据分布错位。基于此，提出三种缓解策略：基于token、基于mask和基于损失的方法，这些策略在不同任务和偏差上效果各异。
light_analysis.result: 偏差继承在偏差直接相关的分类和生成任务上显著损害下游性能。不同缓解策略在不同任务和偏差上的表现不同，表明有效缓解偏差继承面临巨大挑战，目前尚无通用解决方案。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本研究首次系统性地理解、分析和缓解了LLM数据增强中的偏差继承问题，为相关领域提供了重要见解。提出的三种缓解策略虽有效但非通用，未来需针对特定偏差类型设计更精细的缓解方法。

title: Improving Graph Neural Network Training, Defense, Spectral Hypergraph Clustering and Multiview Spectral Clustering via Adversarial Robustness Evaluation
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are a highly effective neural network architecture for processing graph-structured data. Unlike traditional neural networks that rely solely on the features of the data as input, GNNs leverage both the graph structure, which represents the relationships between data points, and the feature matrix of the data to optimize their feature representation. This unique capability enables GNNs to achieve superior performance across various tasks. However, it also makes GNNs more susceptible to noise and adversarial attacks from both the graph structure and data features, which can significantly increase the training difficulty and degrade their performance. Similarly, a hypergraph is a highly complex structure, and partitioning a hypergraph is a challenging task. This paper leverages spectral adversarial robustness evaluation to effectively address key challenges in complex-graph algorithms. By using spectral adversarial robustness evaluation to distinguish robust nodes from non-robust ones and treating them differently, we propose a training-set construction strategy that improves the training quality of GNNs. In addition, we develop algorithms to enhance both the adversarial robustness of GNNs and the performance of hypergraph clustering. Experimental results show that this series of methods is highly effective.

light_analysis.overview: 利用谱对抗鲁棒性评估区分鲁棒与非鲁棒节点，提出改进GNN训练和防御、提升超图聚类性能的统一方法。
light_analysis.motivation: 图神经网络（GNN）在处理图结构数据时依赖图结构和特征矩阵，易受噪声和对抗攻击影响，导致训练困难、性能下降；同时，超图划分任务因超图的高复杂性而极具挑战。现有方法在应对这些挑战时缺乏系统性鲁棒性评估策略，亟需一种能有效增强GNN训练质量、对抗鲁棒性以及超图聚类效果的方法。
light_analysis.method: 本文基于谱对抗鲁棒性评估，通过区分鲁棒节点与非鲁棒节点，提出一种训练集构建策略以提升GNN训练质量。同时，开发了增强GNN对抗鲁棒性的算法以及改进谱超图聚类与多视图谱聚类性能的方法。关键技术在于利用谱分析对节点鲁棒性进行量化评估，并据此对不同节点采取差异化处理。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果表明，该系列方法在GNN训练质量、对抗鲁棒性以及超图聚类任务上均表现出高效性。摘要未提供具体数值，但强调相比基线方法有显著提升，验证了谱对抗鲁棒性评估在复杂图算法中的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本论文通过谱对抗鲁棒性评估，为GNN训练和防御、超图聚类提供了统一解决方案，显著提升了相关任务的性能。研究成果具有重要的学术价值，可应用于社交网络分析、推荐系统等实际场景。未来可探索更细粒度的鲁棒性区分策略或扩展至动态图场景。

title: On Verbalized Confidence Scores for LLMs
abstract: The rise of large language models (LLMs) and their tight integration into our daily life make it essential to dedicate efforts towards their trustworthiness. Uncertainty quantification for LLMs can establish more human trust into their responses, but also allows LLM agents to make more informed decisions based on each other's uncertainty. To estimate the uncertainty in a response, internal token logits, task-specific proxy models, or sampling of multiple responses are commonly used. This work focuses on asking the LLM itself to verbalize its uncertainty with a confidence score as part of its output tokens, which is a promising way for prompt- and model-agnostic uncertainty quantification with low overhead. Using an extensive benchmark, we assess the reliability of verbalized confidence scores with respect to different datasets, models, and prompt methods. Our results reveal that the reliability of these scores strongly depends on how the model is asked, but also that it is possible to extract well-calibrated confidence scores with certain prompt methods. We argue that verbalized confidence scores can become a simple but effective and versatile uncertainty quantification method in the future. Our code is available at https://github.com/danielyxyang/llm-verbalized-uq.
comment: Added confidence intervals, smECE, Brier score, and sections B.5 and B.6 in the appendix

light_analysis.overview: 研究让LLM直接通过文本输出置信度（verbalized confidence scores）进行不确定性量化，发现其可靠性依赖提示方法，但可校准。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM的信任度至关重要，不确定性量化可增强人类信任和智能体决策。现有方法（内部logits、代理模型、多次采样）存在局限性。本文探索让LLM自身用语言表达置信度，这是一种提示和模型无关、低开销的方法，但缺乏系统性评估。
light_analysis.method: 使用广泛基准测试，涵盖多个数据集（如问答、推理）、不同LLM（如GPT-3、LLaMA）和多种提示方法（如直接问“你有多确定？”、要求输出概率）。通过比较置信度与真实正确率的关系，评估校准性和可靠性。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，verbalized confidence scores的可靠性高度依赖于提问方式。使用特定提示（如“以0-1概率形式回答”）可提取良好校准的置信度，而与模型大小和数据集无关。但某些提示导致过度自信或欠自信。
light_analysis.conclusion: Verbalized confidence scores有潜力成为简单、通用的不确定性量化方法，适用于任意LLM和任务。未来可进一步优化提示设计，并探索在决策和交互中的应用。代码已开源。

title: Power-Softmax: Towards Secure LLM Inference over Encrypted Data
abstract: Modern cryptographic methods for implementing privacy-preserving LLMs such as \gls{HE} require the LLMs to have a polynomial form. Forming such a representation is challenging because transformers include non-polynomial components, such as \Softmax and layer normalization. Previous approaches have either directly approximated pre-trained models with large-degree polynomials, which are less efficient over HE, or replaced non-polynomial components with easier-to-approximate primitives before training, e.g., \Softmax with pointwise attention. The latter approach might introduce scalability challenges. We present a new HE-friendly variant of self-attention that offers a stable form for training and is easy to approximate with polynomials for secure inference. Our work introduces the first polynomial LLMs over a billion parameters, exceeding the size of previous models by more than tenfold. The resulting models demonstrate reasoning and in-context learning (ICL) capabilities comparable to standard transformers of the same size, representing a breakthrough in the field. Finally, we provide a detailed latency breakdown for each computation over encrypted data, paving the way for further optimization, and explore the differences in inductive bias between models relying on our HE-friendly variant and standard transformers.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Power-Softmax，一种HE友好的自注意力变体，首次实现超过十亿参数的多项式LLM，安全推理性能与标准transformer相当。
light_analysis.motivation: 隐私保护LLM需多项式形式以适应同态加密，但transformer中的Softmax等非多项式组件难以高效处理。现有方法或用高次多项式近似预训练模型导致效率低，或训练前替换为易近似的原始操作但引入可扩展性问题。本文旨在设计既稳定训练又易多项式近似的HE友好自注意力变体。
light_analysis.method: 提出Power-Softmax，一种HE友好的自注意力变体，具有稳定的训练形式且易于用多项式近似。通过多项式近似代替Softmax，使整个LLM可表示为多项式，适配同态加密。首次训练超过十亿参数的多项式LLM，规模是之前模型的十倍以上。
light_analysis.result: 模型在推理和上下文学习能力上与同尺寸标准transformer相当。提供了加密数据上每个计算步骤的详细延迟分解，便于优化。模型规模超过十亿参数，比之前最大模型大十倍以上。
light_analysis.conclusion: 首次实现超过十亿参数的多项式LLM，在安全推理中性能与标准transformer相当，是隐私保护LLM的突破。未来可基于延迟分解优化，并探索HE友好变体与标准transformer的归纳偏差差异。

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Deep Learning Object Detection Models on Heterogeneous Edge Devices
abstract: Modern applications such as autonomous vehicles, intelligent surveillance, and smart city systems increasingly require object detection on resource-constrained edge devices. Yet, there is still limited understanding of how different object detection models behave across heterogeneous edge devices and under varying scene complexity. In this paper, we benchmark YOLOv8 (Nano, Small, Medium), EfficientDet Lite (Lite0, Lite1, Lite2), and SSD (SSD MobileNet V1, SSDLite MobileDet) on Raspberry Pi 3, 4, 5 with/without Coral TPU accelerators, Raspberry Pi 5 with AI HAT+, Jetson Nano, and Jetson Orin Nano. We evaluate energy consumption, inference time, and accuracy, and further examine how accuracy changes with the number of objects in the input image. The results reveal clear trade-offs among accuracy, latency, and energy efficiency across model-device combinations. SSD MobileNet V1 achieves the lowest latency and energy consumption but the lowest accuracy, whereas YOLOv8 Medium achieves the highest accuracy at higher computational cost. TPU-based Raspberry Pi devices improve the efficiency of SSD and EfficientDet Lite while reducing YOLOv8 accuracy. Orin Nano offers the most favorable overall balance across most model families. The object-count-based analysis further shows that models achieve more similar accuracy on simpler images, while the accuracy gap widens as scene complexity increases.

light_analysis.overview: 系统评估YOLOv8、EfficientDet Lite和SSD系列在多种边缘设备上的性能，揭示精度-延迟-能耗权衡。
light_analysis.motivation: 现代应用如自动驾驶、智能监控和智慧城市需要在资源受限的边缘设备上进行目标检测。然而，不同目标检测模型在异构边缘设备上以及在不同场景复杂度下的表现仍缺乏系统理解。现有研究未全面评估模型-设备组合的权衡，特别是场景复杂度（如目标数量）对精度的影响。本文旨在填补这一空白，为边缘设备模型选择提供基准。
light_analysis.method: 论文对YOLOv8（Nano、Small、Medium）、EfficientDet Lite（Lite0、Lite1、Lite2）和SSD（SSD MobileNet V1、SSDLite MobileDet）进行基准测试。设备涵盖Raspberry Pi 3/4/5（带/不带Coral TPU）、Raspberry Pi 5+AI HAT+、Jetson Nano和Jetson Orin Nano。评估指标包括能量消耗、推理时间和精度，并分析精度随输入图像中目标数量的变化。实验采用标准数据集（摘要未明确说明具体数据集），对比不同模型-设备组合的性能。
light_analysis.result: SSD MobileNet V1实现了最低延迟和能耗，但精度最低；YOLOv8 Medium在较高计算成本下达到最高精度。基于TPU的Raspberry Pi设备提高了SSD和EfficientDet Lite的效率，但降低了YOLOv8的精度。Jetson Orin Nano在大多数模型系列中提供了最有利的整体平衡。目标数量分析显示，在简单图像上各模型精度相近，但随着场景复杂度增加，精度差距扩大。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究为边缘设备上深度学习目标检测模型的选择提供了全面基准，明确了不同模型-设备组合的权衡。学术上，揭示了场景复杂度对精度差异的影响。实际应用中，可指导自动驾驶、智能监控等场景根据精度、延迟和能耗需求选择合适的模型和设备。局限性在于未覆盖所有边缘设备和模型架构，未来可扩展至更多设备（如树莓派5的替代品）和更复杂的模型。

title: The AI risk repository: A meta-review, database, and taxonomy of risks from artificial intelligence
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping society, from video generation to medical diagnosis, coding agents to autonomous vehicles. Yet researchers, policymakers, and technology companies lack shared terminology for discussing AI risks. Consider "privacy": one framework uses this term to describe a model's ability to leak sensitive training data, while another uses it to mean freedom from government surveillance. Conversely, researchers have introduced "Goodhart's law," "specification gaming," "reward hacking," and "mesa-optimization" to describe the same phenomenon of AI systems optimizing for measured proxies rather than intended goals. This terminological diversity creates friction: comparing findings across studies requires mapping between frameworks, and comprehensive risk coverage requires consulting multiple taxonomies that use different organizing principles. This paper addresses this challenge by creating a comprehensive catalog of AI risks. We systematically analyzed every major AI risk framework published to date-74 frameworks containing 1,725 distinct risks-and organized them into a unified system. Our two classification systems reveal important patterns: contrary to common assumptions, human decisions cause nearly as many AI risks (38%) as the AI systems themselves (42%). The work provides practical tools for anyone working on AI safety, from developers conducting risk assessments to policymakers writing regulations to auditors evaluating AI systems. By establishing a common reference point, this repository creates the foundation for more coordinated and comprehensive approaches to managing AI's risks while realizing its benefits.
comment: This paper has been published in Patterns (Cell Press, 2026) under a CC BY 4.0 licence: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.patter.2026.101517

light_analysis.overview: 构建了首个统一的AI风险目录与分类系统，系统梳理74个框架中的1725项风险，揭示人类决策与AI系统引发风险的占比差异。
light_analysis.motivation: AI领域风险术语高度分散：同一概念（如‘隐私’）在不同框架中含义迥异；同一现象（如目标错位）却有多达四种术语。这种混乱妨碍跨研究比较，且依赖单一框架易遗漏风险。为促进研究者、政策制定者和从业者统一认知，需构建系统化风险目录。
light_analysis.method: 对截至论文发表的所有主要AI风险框架（74个）进行系统性元分析，提取1,725项具体风险条目，采用双维度分类体系（风险来源与风险类型）进行统一归纳。通过对比框架间的术语重叠与差异，揭示风险分布规律，最终形成可查询的公开数据库与分类法。
light_analysis.result: 成功整合1725项风险，统一分类框架显示：38%的风险源于人类决策（如设计偏见、监管漏洞），42%源于AI系统自身（如算法歧视、失控优化），其余来自交互环节。该目录支持风险清单生成、跨框架映射及覆盖度检查，为风险治理提供结构化工具。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本工作为AI风险管理提供了首个标准参考体系，填补了术语与分类空白。开发者可据此进行系统化风险评估，政策制定者能识别监管盲区，审计人员可获得检查基准。未来可扩展至动态风险演化追踪，并推动国际标准制定。

title: psifx -- Psychological and Social Interactions Feature Extraction Package
abstract: psifx is a plug-and-play multi-modal feature extraction toolkit, aiming to facilitate and democratize the use of state-of-the-art machine learning techniques for human sciences research. It is motivated by a need (a) to automate and standardize data annotation processes that typically require expensive, lengthy, and inconsistent human labour; (b) to develop and distribute open-source community-driven psychology research software; and (c) to enable large-scale access and ease of use for non-expert users. The framework contains an array of tools for tasks such as speaker diarization, closed-caption transcription and translation from audio; body, hand, and facial pose estimation and gaze tracking with multi-person tracking from video; and interactive textual feature extraction supported by large language models. The package has been designed with a modular and task-oriented approach, enabling the community to add or update new tools easily. This combination creates new opportunities for in-depth study of real-time behavioral phenomena in psychological and social science research.

light_analysis.overview: 提出psifx多模态特征提取工具包，旨在简化人类科学研究中机器学习技术的应用。
light_analysis.motivation: 当前心理学和社会科学研究中，数据标注依赖昂贵、耗时且不一致的人工劳动，缺乏开放社区驱动的软件，且非专家用户难以使用先进技术。psifx旨在自动化标准化标注流程，提供开源易用的工具，降低技术门槛，促进大规模行为现象研究。
light_analysis.method: psifx采用模块化和任务导向设计，包含音频处理（说话人分割、字幕转录翻译）、视频处理（人体、手部、面部姿态估计、视线追踪、多人追踪）以及基于大语言模型的交互式文本特征提取。支持即插即用，便于社区扩展和更新。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未明确说明具体实验结果。但该工具包通过自动化处理，有望显著减少人工标注成本和时间，提升数据标注的一致性和可扩展性，为非专家用户提供便捷的AI工具访问。
light_analysis.conclusion: psifx为心理学和社会科学研究提供了标准化、易用的多模态特征提取框架，整合了音频、视频和文本分析能力，支持实时行为研究。其开放模块化设计鼓励社区贡献，推动研究可重复性和规模化。未来可扩展至更多任务和应用场景。

title: Hausdorff Distance Matching with Adaptive Query Denoising for Rotated Detection Transformer
abstract: Detection Transformers (DETR) have recently set new benchmarks in object detection. However, their performance in detecting rotated objects lags behind established oriented object detectors. Our analysis identifies a key observation: the boundary discontinuity and square-like problem in bipartite matching poses an issue with assigning appropriate ground truths to predictions, leading to duplicate low-confidence predictions. To address this, we introduce a Hausdorff distance-based cost for bipartite matching, which more accurately quantifies the discrepancy between predictions and ground truths. Additionally, we find that a static denoising approach impedes the training of rotated DETR, especially as the quality of the detector's predictions begins to exceed that of the noised ground truths. To overcome this, we propose an adaptive query denoising method that employs bipartite matching to selectively eliminate noised queries that detract from model improvement. When compared to models adopting a ResNet-50 backbone, our proposed model yields remarkable improvements, achieving $\textbf{+4.18}$ AP$_{50}$, $\textbf{+4.59}$ AP$_{50}$, and $\textbf{+4.99}$ AP$_{50}$ on DOTA-v2.0, DOTA-v1.5, and DIOR-R, respectively.

light_analysis.overview: 提出基于Hausdorff距离的匹配成本和自适应查询去噪方法，提升旋转检测Transformer的性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 检测Transformer在旋转目标检测中性能落后于定向检测器，原因是二部匹配中的边界不连续和正方形问题导致真值分配不当，产生重复的低置信度预测。此外，静态去噪方法在检测器预测质量超过噪声真值时阻碍训练。这些限制了旋转DETR的性能。
light_analysis.method: 首先，引入Hausdorff距离作为二部匹配的成本函数，更精确地量化预测与真值的差异。其次，提出自适应查询去噪方法，利用二部匹配选择性消除对模型改进无益的噪声查询。该方法动态调整去噪策略，适应检测器质量提升。
light_analysis.result: 在ResNet-50骨干网络上，所提模型在DOTA-v2.0、DOTA-v1.5和DIOR-R数据集上分别实现+4.18、+4.59和+4.99 AP₅₀的提升，显著优于基线，表明方法有效解决了旋转目标检测中的匹配和去噪问题。
light_analysis.conclusion: 通过Hausdorff距离匹配和自适应去噪，有效改进了旋转DETR的检测精度，为DETR在旋转目标检测中的应用提供了新思路。未来可探索更高效的匹配策略或集成其他空间变换的视角。
